calendar.matters:
MOTION - Notice Of Motion And Motion For Judgment On Petition To Confirm Arbitration Award
Off Calendar Oct-01-2019 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
G. Ryan Patrick, Esq. 415-552-8230 of Wiegel Law Group for Plaintiff.
No other Appearance.

RULING - The matter is Uncontested. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PETITIONER WIEGEL LAW GROUP, PLC's Motion For Judgment On Petition To Confirm Arbitration Award.

Petitioner Wiegel Law Group, PLC's motion for judgment on petition to confirm arbitration award is granted. Respondent Larry Odbert did not properly file or notice for hearing his purported cross-petition to correct contractual arbitration award for hearing. However, it raises the same issues as those in his opposition to petitioner's motion, and is denied.

Respondent fails to show that the arbitrator exceeded her powers within the meaning of Code Civ. Proc. 1286.2(a)(4) or 1286.6(b). Even if the arbitrator misinterpreted the parties' agreement, to the extent this simply constitutes an error of law, it is not a ground to vacate the award. (See Gueyffier v. Ann Summers, Ltd. (2008) 43 Cal.3d 1179, 1184 ["When parties contract to resolve their disputes by private arbitration, their agreement ordinarily contemplates that the arbitrator will have the power to decide any question of contract interpretation, historical fact or general law necessary, in the arbitrator's understanding of the case, to reach a decision. [Citations.] Inherent in that power is the possibility the arbitrator may err in deciding some aspect of the case. Arbitrators do not ordinarily exceed their contractually created powers simply by reaching an erroneous conclusion on a contested issue of law or fact, and arbitral awards may not ordinarily be vacated because of such error, for '"[t]he arbitrator's resolution of these issues is what the parties bargained for in the arbitration.'""]; Moncharsh v. Heily & Blase (1992) 3 Cal.4th 1, 11 ["[I]t is the general rule that, with narrow exceptions, an arbitrator's decision cannot be reviewed for errors of fact or law."].) Moreover, the arbitration award reveals on its face that the arbitrator awarded interest on the fees at the rate of 12% simple interest, the rate of interest called for by the parties' agreement, which does not exceed the legal rate authorized by Civil Code 1916.12-1.

Order and Judgment are signed. (see scanned order and judgment for details)

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Not Reported. =(302/EPS)
